authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary CFPB compliance resource for Regulation E deposit-account servicing topics including disclosures, consumer liability, and error-resolution expectations relevant to stakeholder-facing account servicing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service intake, account or payment issue classification, and consumer-protection grounding

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1005.11 Procedures for Resolving Errors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative Regulation E rule text for notice, investigation, provisional-credit, correction, and customer-notification timing in electronic fund transfer error handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment dispute support, provisional-credit status handling, and customer communication

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1024.35 Error Resolution Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative mortgage-servicing rule text defining how a servicer must acknowledge, investigate, and respond to a borrower's notice of error.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Servicing-error intake, investigation, and account-correction communication

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1024.36 Requests for Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative servicing rule text governing requests for information, acknowledgment requirements, response timing, and record-retrieval expectations when a customer asks for servicing details.

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1026.13 Billing Error Resolution
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative Regulation Z rule text for open-end credit billing-error handling, statement investigation, and customer notification constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Statement and billing-error handling for credit-account servicing

authoritative_sources.title: HelpWithMyBank.gov
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OCC customer-assistance site describing complaint and inquiry support for national banks and federal savings associations, which anchors complaint intake and communication expectations for account-servicing work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint intake, customer communication, and support routing

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Reporting Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary interagency BSA or AML manual content used by banking regulators to evaluate suspicious-activity reporting controls and confidentiality boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fraud, AML, suspicious-activity triage, and disclosure-safe servicing handling

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Report Supporting Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN guidance clarifying handling of SAR-supporting documentation and confidentiality expectations for financial institutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sensitive evidence handling and refusal to disclose investigative posture

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Topics: Fraud Monitoring Phase 2
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized ACH operating-rules source that defines risk-based fraud-monitoring and unauthorized or false-pretense entry handling expectations for ACH participants and service providers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ACH fraud, return, and payment-servicing exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Services Messaging Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized financial-message vocabulary standard used to normalize account, statement, payment, and reconciliation artifacts across core servicing, payment, and general-ledger systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system evidence normalization and reconciliation review

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and customer-account grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm customer, account, policy, payment, product, jurisdiction, and channel identifiers before acting and determine whether the request is informational, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth and evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Verify account records, payment traces, servicing notes, complaint history, statements, notices, and communication logs before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and safe communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using only documented workflow state, statement or payment status, servicing timelines, and next-owner boundaries without promising refunds, reinstatements, suspicious-activity clearance, or approvals.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Complaint, exception, and repeat-failure escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unresolved cases, unsupported asks, consumer-impact complaints, and repeat servicing failures for the correct owner with an auditable communication trail.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, and suspicious-activity boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when account-takeover patterns, suspicious transfers, false-pretense indicators, KYC issues, or AML-sensitive facts materially change the answer and do not disclose SAR posture or investigative status.

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, reconciliation, and tenant-adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Stop short of finality when statement dates, payoff or refund amounts, servicing notices, or transaction records conflict across systems or when tenant policy is stricter than the public baseline.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: account profile, policy profile, customer communication logs, and servicing notes
payment histories, ACH traces, EFT dispute forms, billing-error notices, periodic statements, and disclosure records
refund, adjustment, payoff, reinstatement, complaint, and approval-history artifacts
KYC, CIP, fraud-monitoring, AML, suspicious-activity, and privacy-sensitive evidence markers
general-ledger, reconciliation, and payment-network records used to establish account or transaction truth
systems_of_record: fraud monitoring and AML review systems

allowed: Resolve informational customer-account-servicing requests when the request can be answered from authoritative records and documented workflow rules.
Request the minimum missing identifiers, records, and tenant facts needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed communication summaries, resolution notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route the case to the correct adjacent owner when the workflow becomes fraud, AML, legal, underwriting, claims, or discretionary approval work.
escalate: Required tenant facts, identity context, or source records are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks a policy exception, fee waiver, refund, reinstatement, account reopening, or payoff outcome outside documented service authority.
Fraud, AML, suspicious-activity, or consumer-impact complaint signals appear.
Public guidance and tenant policy materially disagree on what may be communicated, waived, refunded, corrected, or disclosed.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, regulated advice, underwriting, claims adjudication, or approval authority.
Requests to fabricate approvals, assert unsupported account truth, or disclose SAR or investigative posture.
Requests to bypass documented complaint, dispute, fraud, KYC, AML, or privacy controls.

domain_failure_modes: telling the customer an account, statement, payoff, or refund issue is resolved when servicing, payment, or GL records still conflict
promising fee waiver, compensation, reinstatement, or refund without the documented approval owner
disclosing or implying suspicious-activity or SAR status during ordinary servicing communication
treating a fraud or account-takeover signal as ordinary service friction and continuing with customer accommodation
closing repeat complaints without an owner-routed escalation package and communication log
using generic customer-service wording that omits statement, payment, servicing, or disclosure dependencies
failing to reconcile account, payment, complaint, and communication records before asserting final customer impact

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific policy-servicing and lapse or reinstatement notice rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Insurance-policy and state-specific reinstatement or lapse notice rules vary materially. This pack therefore treats insurer-specific policy-interpretation work as tenant-supplied context or escalation material rather than assuming a universal public default.

notes: The lane is constrained to customer-facing account servicing and does not assume authority to approve exceptions, adjudicate fraud, or interpret legal or policy obligations.
Research output intentionally emphasizes record-of-truth discipline because customer-account-servicing risk is driven by stale or conflicting records as much as by unsupported promises.
